message:
refactor(core): simplify Option chains in identity, setup, parse_token_line

Three Option-handling patterns that read as clippy-style noise.
Collapse them in place; no behavior change.

- \`resolve_project_identity\` in identity.rs
  Two places used
  \`and_then(|x| if cond(&x) { Some(x) } else { None })\`
  which is the canonical \`Option::filter\` shape. Swap both for
  \`.filter(|x| cond(x))\`:
  - branch filter on \`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD\` output
    so \`HEAD\` (detached head) reads as absent.
  - the \`project_uid.is_some() || product_uid.is_some()\` gate
    on the \`.acfs-mailbox.toml\` probe.

- \`parse_http_url_for_status\` in setup.rs
  Was a three-arm \`if / else if / else → return None\` that
  assigned \`(scheme, rest, port)\` in the http arm and mirrored
  the shape in the https arm. Fold the https arm to
  \`let rest = trimmed.strip_prefix(\"https://\")?;\` and return
  \`(\"https\", rest, 443_u16)\` so the \`None\` propagation
  comes from the same \`?\` operator the http arm would have used.
  The final triple is bound from whichever arm matched.

- \`parse_token_line\` in toon.rs
  Same simplification for the arrow search: the \`->\` arm stays
  as an explicit branch because its payload is a plain byte slice
  (not an Option), but the unicode-arrow (\'\u{2192}\') fallback
  becomes \`let pos = trimmed.find(\'\u{2192}\')?\`; on miss the
  function early-returns \`None\` exactly as before.

These are literal mechanical translations — both the
\`Option::filter\` and \`?\`-on-find/strip_prefix forms compile to
the same MIR as the hand-written conditionals the clippy lint
suggests replacing. No tests touched.
